# Contributing to arro-server

Thank you for your interest in contributing to **arro-server**, an open-source project maintained by **GENEFOLD AI LTD**.

---

## Open-Source & Voluntary Contributions

`arro-server` is free and open-source software licensed under the [Apache License, Version 2.0](../LICENSE).

**Contributions are accepted on a voluntary basis only.**  
By submitting any contribution (code, documentation, tests, bug reports, suggestions, or any other material) to this repository, you acknowledge and agree that:

1. **No compensation or employment relationship.** No contributor is entitled to payment, remuneration, equity, or any other form of compensation from GENEFOLD AI LTD in connection with contributions made to this project. Contributing does not create an employment, contractor, partnership, or agency relationship of any kind.

2. **No claims on contributions.** Once submitted and merged, contributions become part of the project and are released under the Apache-2.0 License. Contributors waive any claim to exclusive ownership over their contribution as it relates to the combined Work. You retain credit as an author in the project's commit history.

3. **IP assignment.** Unless you explicitly state otherwise in writing at the time of submission, any Contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in this Work shall be licensed to GENEFOLD AI LTD and all downstream recipients under the terms of the Apache License, Version 2.0, without additional terms or conditions. This is consistent with Section 5 of the Apache-2.0 License.

4. **Originality.** You confirm that your Contribution is your original work, or that you have the necessary rights to submit it, and that it does not knowingly infringe any third-party intellectual property rights.

5. **No warranty.** Contributions are provided "AS IS", without warranty of any kind. GENEFOLD AI LTD reserves the right to accept, reject, modify, or remove any contribution at its sole discretion.

---

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Issues

- Search [existing issues](https://github.com/Genefold/arro-server/issues) before opening a new one.
- Use a clear, descriptive title.
- Include steps to reproduce, expected behaviour, actual behaviour, and your environment (Python version, OS, relevant package versions).

### Submitting a Pull Request

1. **Fork** the repository and create a branch from `main`.
2. **Follow the existing code style.** The project uses `ruff` for linting (`ruff check .` and `ruff format .`).
3. **Write or update tests** for any changed behaviour. Run `pytest -q` before opening your PR.
4. **Keep PRs focused.** One logical change per PR makes review faster and keeps history clean.
5. **Fill in the PR template** completely. The checklist includes an explicit acceptance of this document and the LICENSE.
6. Open your PR against the `main` branch.

### Commit Message Convention

Use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/):

```
<type>(<scope>): <short summary>

[optional body]
[optional footer]
```

Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `refactor`, `perf`.

### Code of Conduct

All contributors are expected to engage respectfully. Harassment, discrimination, or disruptive behaviour will not be tolerated and may result in removal from the project.

---

## Questions?

Open a [GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/Genefold/arro-server/discussions) or file an issue tagged `question`.

---

*GENEFOLD AI LTD reserves the right to update these contribution terms at any time. The version in effect at the time of your submission applies to that submission.*
